```html app/egreen/templates/admin/index.html {% extends "admin/index.html" %} {% load i18n %} {% block content %}

{% translate "Custom features" %}

{% translate "Launch tasks" %} {% translate "All exports" %} {% translate "Tasks queue" %} {% translate "Terdecree setup page" %}
{{ block.super }} {% endblock %} ``` In django admin template how to provent user non staff to access this section To prevent non-staff users from accessing this section in the Django admin template, you can use the `is_staff` check. Here's how to modify the template: ```html {% extends "admin/index.html" %} {% load i18n %} {% block content %} {% if user.is_staff %} {# Add this check #}

{% translate "Custom features" %}

{% translate "Launch tasks" %} {% translate "All exports" %} {% translate "Tasks queue" %} {% translate "Terdecree setup page" %}
{% endif %} {{ block.super }} {% endblock %} ``` However, it's important to note that this is just a UI-level restriction. You should also: 1. Ensure that the views handling these URLs are properly protected using Django's decorators or mixins: ```python from django.contrib.admin.views.decorators import staff_member_required @staff_member_required def your_view(request): # your view logic here pass ``` Or for class-based views: ```python from django.contrib.admin.views.decorators import staff_member_required from django.utils.decorators import method_decorator @method_decorator(staff_member_required, name='dispatch') class YourView(View): # your view logic here pass ``` 2. You can also use the `@user_passes_test` decorator for more specific permissions: ```python from django.contrib.auth.decorators import user_passes_test def staff_check(user): return user.is_staff @user_passes_test(staff_check) def your_view(request): # your view logic here pass ``` Remember that template-level restrictions are not enough for security - always implement proper view-level permissions to ensure secure access control.
